purpose: First organic-corpus benchmark for trusty-search v0.10.0 per-lane MCP tools (#5). The 47-file synthetic corpus (#123 v2) was too small to differentiate search_kg from search_semantic — KG-leading collapsed to hybrid because of low inter-file call density. open-mpm is ~282 source files of real Rust code authored before these benchmark queries existed, so it answers: does the KG signal pull its weight on an organic Rust workspace?
schema_notes: Each query carries a `type` (definition / conceptual / kg_seed / negative), a `mode_hint` (code / text / data), and `ground_truth_files` (paths relative to the open-mpm crate root). KG-seed queries additionally carry `kg_seed_query` — a lexical query string used in stage-1 to discover the seed chunk_id before stage-2 runs search_kg.

categories.definition: Single distinctive identifier — caller wants the file where it is defined.
categories.conceptual: Multi-word phrase describing what the code does, not the identifier itself.
categories.kg_seed: Two-stage KG-relevant query: stage-1 lexical seeds the chunk_id, stage-2 search_kg returns connected chunks. Designed to exercise the symbol graph specifically (callers / callees / contains).
categories.negative: Symbol that does NOT exist in open-mpm. All tools should return zero/empty results gracefully.

queries.description: PascalCase struct that implements the AgentRunner trait by spawning a CLI subprocess. Exact symbol — lexical lane should hit.
PascalCase struct holding the on-disk catalogue of MPM sessions.
PascalCase struct managing MCP plugin lifecycle (start / stop / status).
snake_case function: lock + tmp + rename atomic write helper. Tests #119 snake_case Definition routing on an organic corpus.
snake_case function computing per-call LLM cost in USD from token usage. Another snake_case definition probe.
SCREAMING_SNAKE constant — 512KB hard cap on read-file tool inputs. Expected to MISS on Hit@1 until #142+#143 land (constant-Definition routing); useful baseline data point.
SCREAMING_SNAKE constant — the tool-name string for the finish_task tool. Second SCREAMING_SNAKE probe.

queries.text: broadcast events to subscribers of a process-wide channel
queries.description: Describes the events module's broadcast::Sender pattern without naming it. No literal identifier overlap; semantic lane should win.

queries.text: spawn a CLI subagent and capture its stdout output
queries.description: Conceptual description of spawn_subagent_and_run. The literal words 'spawn'/'subagent'/'stdout' do appear in the file but not as a phrase.

queries.text: atomic file write using lock and rename for crash safety
queries.description: Conceptual description of atomic_write / atomic_append_line. Words like 'crash-safe' / 'lock' appear in module docs but not all together.

queries.text: compute reciprocal rank fusion of hybrid search lanes
queries.description: Describes the RRF fusion step. Tests whether semantic retrieval can connect a query that uses precise terminology ('reciprocal rank fusion') to the search subsystem.

queries.text: persist conversation turns to disk between agent invocations
queries.description: Multi-file conceptual query about session persistence. Any of these files counts as a hit; tests semantic disambiguation between three related modules.

queries.text: load skill markdown frontmatter and register skill index
queries.description: Conceptual query about skill loading. Multiple plausible files; semantic should rank skills/registry.rs or skills/index.rs at top.

queries.text: dispatch JSON-RPC tool calls to MCP servers over stdio
queries.description: Conceptual query about MCP stdio plumbing. 'dispatch', 'JSON-RPC', 'stdio' don't all appear together; semantic vs lexical comparison probe.

queries.text: compute LLM token usage cost in dollars from input output rates
queries.description: Conceptual description of cost_usd. Tests whether the daemon can route a multi-word description of a single small utility to the right file.

Two-stage KG query. Stage 1: lexical lookup of SubprocessAgentRunner finds the seed chunk in subprocess.rs. Stage 2: search_kg returns chunks connected via calls / called_by / inherits edges — runtime.rs uses it, ctrl/mod.rs dispatches into it, sibling AgentRunner impls. Ground truth is the union of plausible KG neighbours.

Two-stage KG query. Stage 1: lexical lookup of ToolRegistry finds the seed in tools/mod.rs. Stage 2: search_kg returns the call neighbourhood — register_tm_tools mutates it, in_process_runner consumes it, runtime constructs it. Ground truth is the call/use neighbourhood.

Two-stage KG query starting at a SCREAMING_SNAKE constant. Stage 1: lexical finds EVENT_LINE_PREFIX (defined in events.rs). Stage 2: search_kg should reach the call sites that prefix event lines on stdout (subprocess.rs, ipc/mod.rs).

Negative case: this symbol does not exist anywhere in open-mpm. All four tools should return zero or near-zero results gracefully — the harness asserts no false-positive ground-truth match (vacuously true) and prints the top-1 file for diagnostic purposes.

Negative conceptual case: this is a query string from the synthetic corpus (Q08 of glyphwarpen-observatory) which should have zero meaningful matches in open-mpm. Validates that semantic similarity does not hallucinate spurious matches when no relevant code exists.
